title: 点群アニメーションの変更です。1. カメラを動かさなくていいので、斜めから見て3Dであることがわかるようにする。VLMのネットワーク学習様子にして。今は単なるニューラルネットワークなので

<system-reminder>
The tools listed below are available in this environment, but their schemas may be omitted from the current tool list to save context.
Only use ToolSearch for tools that appear in the Deferred tools list below. The only valid ToolSearch names are the exact strings before the colon on each list entry. Use query "select:<name>[,<name>...]" to load one or more of those listed tools before calling them.
Do NOT call ToolSearch for tools that are already present in the current tool list, including core tools like Read, Grep, Glob, LS, Execute, ApplyPatch, AskUser, Create, Edit, TodoWrite, or ExitSpecMode. Call available tools directly.
Do NOT guess tool names or use aliases, invent MCP namespaces, or search for tools not shown in the Deferred tools list. If a needed tool is neither currently available nor listed below, it is unavailable in this session; do not retry ToolSearch for it. Calling an omitted tool directly will fail with InputValidationError.

IMPORTANT: When a user's task matches one of these tools and the tool is absent from the current tool list, load and use it rather than routing around it via Execute. Examples of routing violations to avoid:
- Using curl, wget, or gh pr view <url> instead of loading and calling FetchUrl
- Using gh search or scraping search engines instead of loading and calling WebSearch
- Hitting MCP server HTTP endpoints via curl instead of the corresponding MCP tool

<system-reminder>
Available skills for the Skill tool are listed below. The Skill tool definition relies on this system reminder for valid skill names.

Skills instructions:
When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any available skill can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge.

How to use skills:
- Invoke skills using the Skill tool with the skill name only.
- The skill's prompt will expand and provide detailed instructions on how to complete the task.
- Only use skills listed in the available-skills system reminders or dynamic skill-discovery reminders.
- Do not invoke a skill that is already running.

Available skills:
agent-browser: Automates browsers and Electron desktop apps (VS Code, Slack, Discord, Figma, Notion, Spotify, etc.) for testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction. Use when the user needs to navigate, interact with, test, or extract data from any website or Electron desktop app.
tuistory: Automates terminal user interface (TUI) testing. Use when you need to launch, interact with, test, or debug terminal applications, capture TUI snapshots, or automate terminal inputs.
figma-mcp-helper: Promote and assist with Figma MCP integration. ACTIVATE when the user shares a Figma URL (figma.com), mentions Figma designs or components, shares PNG images that may originate from Figma, or when Figma MCP tools are already connected and being used. Handles installation encouragement, conversational promotion, and push-back-to-Figma flows.
install-code-review: Install and configure Factory Droid for automated code review on GitHub or GitLab. Supports single-repo setup or org/group-wide rollout across hundreds of repos. Use when a user wants to set up Droid review on their repositories.
install-triage: Scaffold a scheduled Slack triage automation, generalized to any company. Sets up a Python tool layer (run_triage.py) plus a HEARTBEAT.md agent loop that scans configured Slack channels for actionable messages, dedupes against a ticketing system (Linear or Jira), files tickets, and posts a run summary. Use when the user wants to stand up an automated triage bot.
review: Review code changes and identify high-confidence, actionable bugs. Use when the user wants to: - Review a pull request or branch diff - Find bugs, security issues, or correctness problems in code changes - Get a structured summary of review findings
simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
session-navigation: Navigate, search, and manage Droid sessions. Use when the user wants to: - List recent sessions - Search session history for specific topics or patterns - Resume a previous session - Get details about what was accomplished in a session - Find sessions by project, date, or content
pdf-document: Produce polished PDF documents (reports, invoices, resumes, letters, flyers, certificates, any "export to PDF" deliverable). Use whenever the user asks for a PDF or a printable document.
powerpoint: Produce polished PowerPoint presentations (decks, slide shows, pitch decks, any "export to PowerPoint" deliverable). Use whenever the user asks for a PowerPoint, a slide deck, or a presentation.
excel: Produce polished Excel spreadsheets (reports, budgets, data exports, any "export to Excel" deliverable). Use whenever the user asks for an Excel file, a spreadsheet, or an .xlsx deliverable.
install-qa: Set up automated QA testing for this project. Performs deep codebase analysis, asks targeted questions, and generates a modular QA skill with sub-skills per app, a GitHub Actions workflow, and a report template. This is a complex, multi-phase process -- quality assurance is foundational and we take the time to get it right.
security-review: Security-focused code review using STRIDE, OWASP Top 10, OWASP LLM Top 10, and supply chain analysis. Use when: - Reviewing a PR for security vulnerabilities - Performing a security audit of code changes - Identifying injection, auth, data exposure, and other security issues - Running a full-project security audit reviewing every source file
deep-security-review: Correctness-first, depth-first security audit of a single repository. Invoked by /security-review when the user opts into "thorough" mode. Uses a heterogeneous multi-model jury (latest Opus + latest GPT + latest Gemini). A mandatory 3-pass floor (line-anchor verification, vendor prior-art, deep prior-art) runs against every Pass 0 lieutenant-seeded candidate; a conditional escalation tier (dataflow + reachability, exploit construction, adversarial red-team disprove) fires on per-finding triggers. Produces FINDINGS.md, JUDGE.md, STATUS.md, severity-sorted master list, and optional PoC + evidence artifacts for that repo. Never uploads or submits anything; all outputs local.
wiki: Generate comprehensive codebase documentation for a repository. Uploads the wiki to view in the Factory app.
wiki-video-gen: Generate Factory-branded HyperFrames video overviews for repository wikis. Use when wiki generation reaches Phase 3.6, or when the user asks for a narrated repository overview video.
install-wiki: Install a CI action that automatically refreshes the Factory Wiki on each push to the default branch. Use when the user wants to set up automated wiki generation, install wiki CI, or configure wiki refresh.
browse-wiki: Search and read wiki documentation for a repository
incident: RCA runbook for alerts. Given an alert link (or prompted to provide one), identifies the alert type, verifies tooling/auth, and walks through root cause analysis using deep research. Persists learnings to incident-guidelines for future reuse.
durable-objects: Create and review Cloudflare Durable Objects. Use when building stateful coordination (chat rooms, multiplayer games, booking systems), implementing RPC methods, SQLite storage, alarms, WebSockets, or reviewing DO code for best practices. Covers Workers integration, wrangler config, and testing with Vitest. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
wrangler: Cloudflare Workers CLI for deploying, developing, and managing Workers, KV, R2, D1, Vectorize, Hyperdrive, Workers AI, Containers, Queues, Workflows, Pipelines, and Secrets Store. Load before running wrangler commands to ensure correct syntax and best practices. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
agents-sdk: Build AI agents on Cloudflare Workers using the Agents SDK. Load when creating stateful agents, durable workflows, real-time WebSocket apps, scheduled tasks, MCP servers, chat applications, voice agents, or browser automation. Covers Agent class, state management, callable RPC, Workflows, durable execution, queues, retries, observability, and React hooks. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
cloudflare: Comprehensive Cloudflare platform skill covering Workers, Pages, storage (KV, D1, R2), AI (Workers AI, Vectorize, Agents SDK), feature flags (Flagship), networking (Tunnel, Spectrum), security (WAF, DDoS), and infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Pulumi). Use for any Cloudflare development task. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
cloudflare-email-service: Send and receive transactional emails with Cloudflare Email Service (Email Sending + Email Routing). Use when building email sending (Workers binding or REST API), email routing, Agents SDK email handling, or integrating email into any app — Workers, Node.js, Python, Go, etc. Also use for email deliverability, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, wrangler email setup, MCP email tools, or when a coding agent needs to send emails. Even for simple requests like "add email to my Worker" — this skill has critical config details.
workers-best-practices: Reviews and authors Cloudflare Workers code against production best practices. Load when writing new Workers, reviewing Worker code, configuring wrangler.jsonc, or checking for common Workers anti-patterns (streaming, floating promises, global state, secrets, bindings, observability). Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
sandbox-sdk: Build sandboxed applications for secure code execution. Load when building AI code execution, code interpreters, CI/CD systems, interactive dev environments, or executing untrusted code. Covers Sandbox SDK lifecycle, commands, files, code interpreter, and preview URLs. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
web-perf: Analyzes web performance using Chrome DevTools MCP. Measures Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS) and supplementary metrics (FCP, TBT, Speed Index), identifies render-blocking resources, network dependency chains, layout shifts, caching issues, and accessibility gaps. Use when asked to audit, profile, debug, or optimize page load performance, Lighthouse scores, or site speed. Biases towards retrieval from current documentation over pre-trained knowledge.
uv-package-manager: Master the uv package manager for fast Python dependency management, virtual environments, and modern Python project workflows. Use when setting up Python projects, managing dependencies, or optimizing Python development workflows with uv.
skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
grill-with-docs: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan against their project's language and documented decisions.
pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
tdd: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.
grill-me: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".
capture: Background knowledge for droid-control workflows -- not invoked directly. Recording lifecycle for terminal and browser sessions.
verify: Background knowledge for droid-control workflows -- not invoked directly. Deliverable verification against commitments.
droid-cli: Background knowledge for droid-control workflows -- not invoked directly. Droid CLI target patterns, shortcuts, modes, and launch helpers.
compose: Background knowledge for droid-control workflows -- not invoked directly. Video assembly via Remotion — title cards, layout, transitions, effects, and showcase polish.
pty-capture: Background knowledge for droid-control workflows -- not invoked directly. Capture ground-truth byte sequences from real terminal emulators.
droid-control: Control terminal TUIs and web/Electron apps for testing, demos, QA, and computer-use tasks. Use when you need to automate a CLI, drive a browser, record a demo, or capture proof artifacts.
desktop-control: Background knowledge for droid-control workflows -- not invoked directly. Desktop-control driver mechanics for native GUI app automation via trycua cua-driver.
showcase: Background knowledge for droid-control workflows -- not invoked directly. Visual polish for videos via Remotion-powered window chrome, animations, and branded backgrounds.
true-input: Background knowledge for droid-control workflows -- not invoked directly. True-input driver mechanics for real terminal emulator automation via headless Wayland compositor.

Proactively invoke a listed skill when the user's request warrants specialized instructions or would benefit from that skill's domain knowledge.
</system-reminder>

```typescript
const generatePatchPattern = () => {
  const patterns = [
    // Circle
    (i, j) => {
      const dy = i - 1.5;
      const dx = j - 1.5;
      return Math.max(0, 1 - Math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) / 2);
    },
    // Cross
    (i, j) => {
      return Math.max(
        Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - 1.5) / 0.5),
        Math.exp(-Math.abs(j - 1.5) / 0.5)
      );
    },
    // Diagonal
    (i, j) => {
      return Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.2);
    },
    // Random
    (i, j) => Math.random() * 0.7 + 0.15,
  ];
  return patterns[Math.floor(Math.random() * patterns.length)];
};
```

```typescript
const TAU = Math.PI * 2;

// Fixed camera — diagonal view showing 3D depth (no rotation).
const CAM_ROT_Y = 0.52;
const CAM_TILT = -0.38;
const FOCAL = 950;
const CAM_Z = 820;

// Training cycle durations.
const FWD_DUR = 3.0;
const BWD_DUR = 2.5;
const UPDATE_DUR = 0.5;
const SETTLE_DUR = 1.0;
const CYCLE_DUR = FWD_DUR + BWD_DUR + UPDATE_DUR + SETTLE_DUR;

// Image patch brightness patterns (simulates different training images).
const PATCH_PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => { // circle
    const dy = i - 1, dx = j - 1;
    return Math.max(0.08, 1 - Math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) / 1.8);
  },
  (i, j) => { // horizontal stripe
    return 0.2 + 0.7 * Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - 1) / 0.6);
  },
  (i, j) => { // diagonal
    return Math.max(0.08, Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.0));
  },
  (i, j) => { // cross
    return Math.max(Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - 1) / 0.5), Math.exp(-Math.abs(j - 1) / 0.5)) * 0.8 + 0.15;
  },
  (i, j) => { // random noise
    return 0.15 + Math.random() * 0.7;
  },
];

const randn = () =>
  (Math.random() + Math.random() + Math.random() - 1.5) * 0.5;

export default function ParticleField() {
  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    const canvas = canvasRef.current;
    if (!canvas) return;
    const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d", { alpha: true });
    if (!ctx) return;

    let animationId = 0;
    let width = 0;
    let height = 0;
    let dpr = 1;
    let resizeTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;

    let nodes: NetNode[][] = [];
    let edges: NetEdge[] = [];
    let runStart = performance.now();
    let nextReset = 28 + Math.random() * 12;
    let cycle = 0;
    let patIdx = Math.floor(Math.random() * PATCH_PATTERNS.length);
    let stepCount = 0;

    let stageSpacingX = 120;
    let ringRadius = 70;
    let patchSpacing = 38;
    let layoutCy = 0;

    // 3D → screen projection with fixed camera.
    const project = (wx: number, wy: number, wz: number) => {
      const cosY = Math.cos(CAM_ROT_Y);
      const sinY = Math.sin(CAM_ROT_Y);
      const rx = wx * cosY - wz * sinY;
      const rz = wx * sinY + wz * cosY;
      const cosT = Math.cos(CAM_TILT);
      const sinT = Math.sin(CAM_TILT);
      const ty = wy * cosT - rz * sinT;
      const tz = wy * sinT + rz * cosT;
      const denom = CAM_Z + tz;
      const safeDenom = denom > 1 ? denom : 1;
      const scale = FOCAL / safeDenom;
      return {
        sx: width / 2 + rx * scale,
        sy: layoutCy + ty * scale,
        depth: tz,
        scale,
      };
    };

    const buildNetwork = () => {
      nodes = [];
      edges = [];

      const availW = width - 280;
      stageSpacingX = Math.max(100, Math.min(140, availW / (NUM_STAGES - 1)));
      const h = height;
      ringRadius = Math.min(68, h * 0.15);
      patchSpacing = Math.min(34, h * 0.075);
      layoutCy = h * 0.5;

      // Stage 0: image patches in a grid (Y-Z plane)
      const patchNodes: NetNode[] = [];
      for (let i = 0; i < PATCH_GRID; i++) {
        for (let j = 0; j < PATCH_GRID; j++) {
          const py = (i - (PATCH_GRID - 1) / 2) * patchSpacing;
          const pz = (j - (PATCH_GRID - 1) / 2) * patchSpacing;
          patchNodes.push({
            stage: 0,
            idx: i * PATCH_GRID + j,
            x: -(NUM_STAGES - 1) / 2 * stageSpacingX,
            y: py,
            z: pz,
            act: 0,
            grad: 0,
            twinkle: Math.random() * TAU,
            brightness: 0.3,
            targetBrightness: 0.3,
          });
        }
      }
      nodes.push(patchNodes);

      // Stages 1-5: rings in Y-Z plane
      for (let s = 1; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        const n = STAGE_SIZES[s];
        const stageNodes: NetNode[] = [];
        const wx = (s - (NUM_STAGES - 1) / 2) * stageSpacingX;
        for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
          const ang = (i / n) * TAU;
          stageNodes.push({
            stage: s,
            idx: i,
            x: wx,
            y: Math.cos(ang) * ringRadius,
            z: Math.sin(ang) * ringRadius,
            act: 0,
            grad: 0,
            twinkle: Math.random() * TAU,
            brightness: 0,
            targetBrightness: 0,
          });
        }
        nodes.push(stageNodes);
      }

      const addEdge = (a: NetNode, b: NetNode, w: number, kind: NetEdge["kind"]) => {
        edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind });
      };

      // Patch → Vision encoder (spatially weighted)
      for (const a of nodes[0]) {
        const ai = Math.floor(a.idx / PATCH_GRID);
        const aj = a.idx % PATCH_GRID;
        for (const b of nodes[1]) {
          // Weight based on patch position relative to encoder node
          const bi = b.idx % PATCH_GRID;
          const bj = Math.floor(b.idx / PATCH_GRID) % PATCH_GRID;
          const dist = Math.sqrt((ai - bi) ** 2 + (aj - bj) ** 2);
          const w = Math.exp(-dist / 1.5) * 0.6 + 0.15;
          addEdge(a, b, w * randn(), "patch");
        }
      }

      // Vision encoder → Projection
      for (const a of nodes[1]) {
        for (const b of nodes[2]) {
          addEdge(a, b, 0.3 + randn() * 0.25, "proj");
        }
      }

      // Projection → Cross-attention (vision branch)
      for (const a of nodes[2]) {
        for (const b of nodes[4]) {
          addEdge(a, b, 0.3 + randn() * 0.25, "cross-v");
        }
      }

      // Text tokens → Cross-attention (language branch)
      for (const a of nodes[3]) {
        for (const b of nodes[4]) {
          addEdge(a, b, 0.3 + randn() * 0.25, "cross-t");
        }
      }

      // Cross-attention → LM decoder
      for (const a of nodes[4]) {
        for (const b of nodes[5]) {
          addEdge(a, b, 0.3 + randn() * 0.25, "lm");
        }
      }

      // Skip connections
      for (let i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
        addEdge(nodes[1][i % 8], nodes[4][i % 8], 0.15 + randn() * 0.1, "skip");
        addEdge(nodes[3][i % 8], nodes[5][i % 8], 0.15 + randn() * 0.1, "skip");
      }
    };

    const newPatchPattern = () => {
      patIdx = (patIdx + 1 + Math.floor(Math.random() * (PATCH_PATTERNS.length - 1))) % PATCH_PATTERNS.length;
      const pat = PATCH_PATTERNS[patIdx];
      for (const n of nodes[0]) {
        const i = Math.floor(n.idx / PATCH_GRID);
        const j = n.idx % PATCH_GRID;
        n.targetBrightness = pat(i, j);
      }
    };

    const resetRun = () => {
      newPatchPattern();
      for (const e of edges) {
        e.tw += (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.08;
      }
      runStart = performance.now();
      nextReset = 28 + Math.random() * 12;
      cycle = 0;
      stepCount++;
    };

    const resize = () => {
      dpr = Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio || 1, 2);
      width = window.innerWidth;
      height = Math.min(380, Math.max(260, Math.floor(window.innerHeight * 0.38)));
      canvas.width = Math.floor(width * dpr);
      canvas.height = Math.floor(height * dpr);
      canvas.style.width = `${width}px`;
      canvas.style.height = `${height}px`;
      ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0);
      ctx.fillStyle = "#000000";
      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);
      buildNetwork();
      newPatchPattern();
    };

    const debouncedResize = () => {
      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);
      resizeTimer = setTimeout(resize, 180);
    };

    const fillCircle = (sx: number, sy: number, r: number, color: string, alpha: number) => {
      if (r < 0.2) return;
      ctx.globalAlpha = alpha;
      ctx.fillStyle = color;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.arc(sx, sy, r, 0, TAU);
      ctx.fill();
    };

    const fillRect = (sx: number, sy: number, size: number, color: string, alpha: number) => {
      if (size < 0.2) return;
      ctx.globalAlpha = alpha;
      ctx.fillStyle = color;
      ctx.fillRect(sx - size / 2, sy - size / 2, size, size);
    };

    const draw = (now: number) => {
      const rt = (now - runStart) * 0.001;
      if (rt > nextReset) resetRun();

      const cycleTime = rt % CYCLE_DUR;
      let phase: "fwd" | "bwd" | "update" | "settle" = "fwd";
      let phaseT = 0;
      if (cycleTime < FWD_DUR) {
        phase = "fwd";
        phaseT = cycleTime / FWD_DUR;
      } else if (cycleTime < FWD_DUR + BWD_DUR) {
        phase = "bwd";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - FWD_DUR) / BWD_DUR;
      } else if (cycleTime < FWD_DUR + BWD_DUR + UPDATE_DUR) {
        phase = "update";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - FWD_DUR - BWD_DUR) / UPDATE_DUR;
      } else {
        phase = "settle";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - FWD_DUR - BWD_DUR - UPDATE_DUR) / SETTLE_DUR;
      }

      const curCycle = Math.floor(rt / CYCLE_DUR);
      if (curCycle !== cycle) {
        cycle = curCycle;
        for (const e of edges) {
          e.tw += (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.04;
        }
      }

      // Update patch brightness (smooth transition)
      for (const n of nodes[0]) {
        n.brightness += (n.targetBrightness - n.brightness) * 0.08;
      }

      // Update edge weights
      const updateRate = phase === "update" ? 0.15 : phase === "settle" ? 0.04 : 0.01;
      for (const e of edges) e.w += (e.tw - e.w) * updateRate;

      // Sweep position for forward/backward
      let sweepPos = 0;
      let sweepDir: "fwd" | "bwd" | "none" = "none";
      if (phase === "fwd") {
        sweepPos = phaseT * (NUM_STAGES - 1);
        sweepDir = "fwd";
      } else if (phase === "bwd") {
        sweepPos = (NUM_STAGES - 1) - phaseT * (NUM_STAGES - 1);
        sweepDir = "bwd";
      }

      // Update node activations
      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        for (const n of nodes[s]) {
          if (phase === "settle") {
            n.act *= 0.82;
            n.grad *= 0.82;
          } else if (phase === "fwd") {
            const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - s);
            const bump = Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.4);
            n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.86, bump);
            n.grad *= 0.9;
          } else if (phase === "bwd") {
            const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - s);
            const bump = Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.4);
            n.grad = Math.max(n.grad * 0.86, bump);
            n.act *= 0.9;
          } else if (phase === "update") {
            n.act *= 0.88;
            n.grad *= 0.88;
          }
        }
      }

      // Fade previous frame
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.42)";
      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);

      // Project all nodes
      const projNodes: Array<{ n: NetNode; sx: number; sy: number; depth: number; scale: number }> = [];
      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        for (const n of nodes[s]) {
          const p = project(n.x, n.y, n.z);
          projNodes.push({ n, sx: p.sx, sy: p.sy, depth: p.depth, scale: p.scale });
        }
      }

      type DrawItem = { depth: number; fn: () => void };
      const drawItems: DrawItem[] = [];

      const isSweeping = sweepDir !== "none";

      // Draw edges
      for (const e of edges) {
        const a = e.a;
        const b = e.b;
        const lo = Math.min(a.stage, b.stage);
        const hi = Math.max(a.stage, b.stage);
        const mag = Math.abs(e.w);

        const pa = project(a.x, a.y, a.z);
        const pb = project(b.x, b.y, b.z);
        const avgDepth = (pa.depth + pb.depth) * 0.5;
        const depthFactor = 1 - Math.min(1, Math.max(0, avgDepth / 700)) * 0.55;
        const avgScale = (pa.scale + pb.scale) * 0.5;

        // Determine edge color based on kind
        let edgeColor: string;
        switch (e.kind) {
          case "patch": edgeColor = C.vision; break;
          case "attn": edgeColor = C.vision; break;
          case "proj": edgeColor = C.proj; break;
          case "cross-v": edgeColor = C.proj; break;
          case "cross-t": edgeColor = C.text; break;
          case "lm": edgeColor = C.lm; break;
          case "skip": edgeColor = C.skip; break;
          default: edgeColor = C.edge;
        }

        let alpha = 0.015 + mag * 0.06;
        let lw = (0.3 + mag * 0.5) * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.5, avgScale));

        if (e.kind === "skip") {
          lw = (0.25 + mag * 0.35) * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.5, avgScale));
        }

        // Sweep highlight
        let pulseColor = "";
        let pulseAlpha = 0;
        if (isSweeping && sweepPos >= lo - 0.15 && sweepPos <= hi + 0.15) {
          const span = hi - lo;
          const edgeProgress = span > 0 ? Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (sweepPos - lo) / span)) : 0.5;
          const prox = Math.sin(
            Math.min(1, Math.max(0, 1 - Math.abs(edgeProgress - 0.5) * 2)) * Math.PI * 0.5,
          );
          alpha += prox * 0.14;
          if (sweepDir === "fwd") {
            pulseColor = C.fwd;
            pulseAlpha = prox * 0.4;
          } else {
            pulseColor = C.bwd;
            pulseAlpha = prox * 0.4;
          }
        }

        // Update flash
        if (phase === "update") {
          alpha += 0.06 * (0.5 + 0.5 * Math.sin(rt * 20 + e.a.idx));
        }

        const finalAlpha = Math.min(1, alpha * depthFactor);
        const isSkip = e.kind === "skip";

        drawItems.push({
          depth: avgDepth,
          fn: () => {
            if (isSkip) {
              ctx.setLineDash(DASH);
            } else {
              ctx.setLineDash([]);
            }
            ctx.globalAlpha = finalAlpha;
            ctx.strokeStyle = edgeColor;
            ctx.lineWidth = lw;
            ctx.beginPath();
            ctx.moveTo(pa.sx, pa.sy);
            ctx.lineTo(pb.sx, pb.sy);
            ctx.stroke();

            // Pulse on edge
            if (pulseAlpha > 0.05 && mag > 0.2) {
              const span = hi - lo;
              const p = span > 0 ? Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (sweepPos - lo) / span)) : 0.5;
              const px = pa.sx + (pb.sx - pa.sx) * p;
              const py = pa.sy + (pb.sy - pa.sy) * p;
              fillCircle(px, py, 1.2 * avgScale, pulseColor, pulseAlpha);
            }
          },
        });
      }

      // Sort and draw edges
      drawItems.sort((x, y) => y.depth - x.depth);
      for (const it of drawItems) it.fn();
      ctx.setLineDash([]);

      // Draw nodes (back to front)
      projNodes.sort((x, y) => y.depth - x.depth);
      for (const { n, sx, sy, scale } of projNodes) {
        const depthFactor = 1 - Math.min(1, Math.max(0, (1 - scale) * 0.8)) * 0.4;

        if (n.stage === 0) {
          // Image patches: render as small squares with brightness
          const size = 7 * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.5, scale));
          const brightness = n.brightness;
          const alpha = (0.25 + brightness * 0.5) * depthFactor;
          
          // Glow when active
          if (n.act > 0.05) {
            fillCircle(sx, sy, size * 1.6, C.vision, n.act * 0.08 * depthFactor);
          }
          
          // Patch square
          ctx.globalAlpha = alpha;
          ctx.fillStyle = C.patch;
          ctx.fillRect(sx - size / 2, sy - size / 2, size, size);
          
          // Border
          ctx.globalAlpha = (0.15 + brightness * 0.2) * depthFactor;
          ctx.strokeStyle = C.vision;
          ctx.lineWidth = 0.5;
          ctx.strokeRect(sx - size / 2, sy - size / 2, size, size);
        } else {
          // Regular nodes: circles
          let nodeColor: string;
          switch (n.stage) {
            case 1: nodeColor = C.vision; break;
            case 2: nodeColor = C.proj; break;
            case 3: nodeColor = C.text; break;
            case 4: nodeColor = C.cross; break;
            case 5: nodeColor = C.lm; break;
            default: nodeColor = C.vision;
          }

          const baseR = 2.5 * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.5, scale));
          const actGlow = Math.max(n.act, n.grad);
          const r = baseR + actGlow * 1.5;

          if (actGlow > 0.05) {
            const glowColor = n.grad > n.act ? C.bwd : C.fwd;
            fillCircle(sx, sy, r * 2.8, glowColor, actGlow * 0.06 * depthFactor);
          }
          fillCircle(sx, sy, r, nodeColor, (0.35 + actGlow * 0.25) * depthFactor);
        }
      }

      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      animationId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);
    };

    resize();
    resetRun();
    window.addEventListener("resize", debouncedResize);
    animationId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);

    return () => {
      window.removeEventListener("resize", debouncedResize);
      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);
      cancelAnimationFrame(animationId);
    };
  }, []);

  return (
    <canvas
      ref={canvasRef}
      aria-hidden="true"
      className="pointer-events-none fixed inset-x-0 top-0 z-0 block"
    />
  );
}
```

```typescript
const generatePatchPattern = () => {
  const patterns = [
    // Circle
    (i, j) => {
      const dy = i - 1.5;
      const dx = j - 1.5;
      return Math.max(0, 1 - Math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) / 2);
    },
    // Cross
    (i, j) => {
      return Math.max(
        Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - 1.5) / 0.5),
        Math.exp(-Math.abs(j - 1.5) / 0.5)
      );
    },
    // Diagonal
    (i, j) => {
      return Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.2);
    },
    // Random
    (i, j) => Math.random() * 0.7 + 0.15,
  ];
  return patterns[Math.floor(Math.random() * patterns.length)];
};
```

```
Settle (6.0 to 7.0s):
  Activity fades
  New image patch pattern generated
```

```typescript
"use client";

import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";

interface NetNode {
  stage: number;
  idx: number;
  x: number;
  y: number;
  z: number;
  act: number;
  grad: number; // gradient/activation for backward pass
  twinkle: number;
  brightness: number;
  targetBrightness: number;
}

interface NetEdge {
  a: NetNode;
  b: NetNode;
  w: number;
  tw: number;
  kind: "patch" | "attn" | "proj" | "cross-v" | "cross-t" | "lm" | "skip";
}

// VLM architecture:
//   stage 0: image patches (3×3 grid)       9  — vision input
//   stage 1: vision encoder (ViT)            8  — self-attention over patches
//   stage 2: projection / adapter            6  — vision→language bridge
//   stage 3: text tokens                     8  — language input
//   stage 4: cross-attention fusion          8  — vision+text merge
//   stage 5: LM decoder                      8  — language generation
const PATCH_GRID = 3;
const STAGE_SIZES = [PATCH_GRID * PATCH_GRID, 8, 6, 8, 8, 8];
const NUM_STAGES = STAGE_SIZES.length;

// Color palette: vision (cool blue) → cross-modal (purple) → language (warm amber)
const C = {
  patch: "hsl(210, 28%, 56%)",
  vision: "hsl(205, 24%, 53%)",
  proj: "hsl(255, 18%, 52%)",
  cross: "hsl(268, 20%, 55%)",
  text: "hsl(35, 32%, 54%)",
  lm: "hsl(28, 28%, 50%)",
  edge: "hsl(215, 18%, 48%)",
  fwd: "hsl(200, 38%, 66%)",
  bwd: "hsl(18, 48%, 60%)",
  skip: "hsl(220, 12%, 42%)",
};
const DASH = [3, 4];
const TAU = Math.PI * 2;

// Fixed camera — diagonal view showing 3D depth (no rotation).
const CAM_ROT_Y = 0.52;
const CAM_TILT = -0.38;
const FOCAL = 950;
const CAM_Z = 820;

// Training cycle durations.
const FWD_DUR = 3.0;
const BWD_DUR = 2.5;
const UPDATE_DUR = 0.5;
const SETTLE_DUR = 1.0;
const CYCLE_DUR = FWD_DUR + BWD_DUR + UPDATE_DUR + SETTLE_DUR;

// Image patch brightness patterns (simulates different training images).
const PATCH_PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => { // circle
    const dy = i - 1, dx = j - 1;
    return Math.max(0.08, 1 - Math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) / 1.8);
  },
  (i, j) => { // horizontal stripe
    return 0.2 + 0.7 * Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - 1) / 0.6);
  },
  (i, j) => { // diagonal
    return Math.max(0.08, Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.0));
  },
  (i, j) => { // cross
    return Math.max(Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - 1) / 0.5), Math.exp(-Math.abs(j - 1) / 0.5)) * 0.8 + 0.15;
  },
  (i, j) => { // random noise
    return 0.15 + Math.random() * 0.7;
  },
];

const randn = () =>
  (Math.random() + Math.random() + Math.random() - 1.5) * 0.5;

export default function ParticleField() {
  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    const canvas = canvasRef.current;
    if (!canvas) return;
    const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d", { alpha: true });
    if (!ctx) return;

    let animationId = 0;
    let width = 0;
    let height = 0;
    let dpr = 1;
    let resizeTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;

    let nodes: NetNode[][] = [];
    let edges: NetEdge[] = [];
    let runStart = performance.now();
    let nextReset = 28 + Math.random() * 12;
    let cycle = 0;
    let patIdx = Math.floor(Math.random() * PATCH_PATTERNS.length);
    let stepCount = 0;

    let stageSpacingX = 120;
    let ringRadius = 70;
    let patchSpacing = 38;
    let layoutCy = 0;

    // 3D → screen projection with fixed camera.
    const project = (wx: number, wy: number, wz: number) => {
      const cosY = Math.cos(CAM_ROT_Y);
      const sinY = Math.sin(CAM_ROT_Y);
      const rx = wx * cosY - wz * sinY;
      const rz = wx * sinY + wz * cosY;
      const cosT = Math.cos(CAM_TILT);
      const sinT = Math.sin(CAM_TILT);
      const ty = wy * cosT - rz * sinT;
      const tz = wy * sinT + rz * cosT;
      const denom = CAM_Z + tz;
      const safeDenom = denom > 1 ? denom : 1;
      const scale = FOCAL / safeDenom;
      return {
        sx: width / 2 + rx * scale,
        sy: layoutCy + ty * scale,
        depth: tz,
        scale,
      };
    };

    const buildNetwork = () => {
      nodes = [];
      edges = [];

      const availW = width - 280;
      stageSpacingX = Math.max(100, Math.min(140, availW / (NUM_STAGES - 1)));
      const h = height;
      ringRadius = Math.min(68, h * 0.15);
      patchSpacing = Math.min(34, h * 0.075);
      layoutCy = h * 0.5;

      // Stage 0: image patches in a grid (Y-Z plane)
      const patchNodes: NetNode[] = [];
      for (let i = 0; i < PATCH_GRID; i++) {
        for (let j = 0; j < PATCH_GRID; j++) {
          const py = (i - (PATCH_GRID - 1) / 2) * patchSpacing;
          const pz = (j - (PATCH_GRID - 1) / 2) * patchSpacing;
          patchNodes.push({
            stage: 0,
            idx: i * PATCH_GRID + j,
            x: -(NUM_STAGES - 1) / 2 * stageSpacingX,
            y: py,
            z: pz,
            act: 0,
            grad: 0,
            twinkle: Math.random() * TAU,
            brightness: 0.3,
            targetBrightness: 0.3,
          });
        }
      }
      nodes.push(patchNodes);

      // Stages 1-5: rings in Y-Z plane
      for (let s = 1; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        const n = STAGE_SIZES[s];
        const stageNodes: NetNode[] = [];
        const wx = (s - (NUM_STAGES - 1) / 2) * stageSpacingX;
        for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
          const ang = (i / n) * TAU;
          stageNodes.push({
            stage: s,
            idx: i,
            x: wx,
            y: Math.cos(ang) * ringRadius,
            z: Math.sin(ang) * ringRadius,
            act: 0,
            grad: 0,
            twinkle: Math.random() * TAU,
            brightness: 0,
            targetBrightness: 0,
          });
        }
        nodes.push(stageNodes);
      }

      const addEdge = (a: NetNode, b: NetNode, w: number, kind: NetEdge["kind"]) => {
        edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind });
      };

      // Patch → Vision encoder (spatially weighted)
      for (const a of nodes[0]) {
        const ai = Math.floor(a.idx / PATCH_GRID);
        const aj = a.idx % PATCH_GRID;
        for (const b of nodes[1]) {
          // Weight based on patch position relative to encoder node
          const bi = b.idx % PATCH_GRID;
          const bj = Math.floor(b.idx / PATCH_GRID) % PATCH_GRID;
          const dist = Math.sqrt((ai - bi) ** 2 + (aj - bj) ** 2);
          const w = Math.exp(-dist / 1.5) * 0.6 + 0.15;
          addEdge(a, b, w * randn(), "patch");
        }
      }

      // Vision encoder → Projection
      for (const a of nodes[1]) {
        for (const b of nodes[2]) {
          addEdge(a, b, 0.3 + randn() * 0.25, "proj");
        }
      }

      // Projection → Cross-attention (vision branch)
      for (const a of nodes[2]) {
        for (const b of nodes[4]) {
          addEdge(a, b, 0.3 + randn() * 0.25, "cross-v");
        }
      }

      // Text tokens → Cross-attention (language branch)
      for (const a of nodes[3]) {
        for (const b of nodes[4]) {
          addEdge(a, b, 0.3 + randn() * 0.25, "cross-t");
        }
      }

      // Cross-attention → LM decoder
      for (const a of nodes[4]) {
        for (const b of nodes[5]) {
          addEdge(a, b, 0.3 + randn() * 0.25, "lm");
        }
      }

      // Skip connections
      for (let i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
        addEdge(nodes[1][i % 8], nodes[4][i % 8], 0.15 + randn() * 0.1, "skip");
        addEdge(nodes[3][i % 8], nodes[5][i % 8], 0.15 + randn() * 0.1, "skip");
      }
    };

    const newPatchPattern = () => {
      patIdx = (patIdx + 1 + Math.floor(Math.random() * (PATCH_PATTERNS.length - 1))) % PATCH_PATTERNS.length;
      const pat = PATCH_PATTERNS[patIdx];
      for (const n of nodes[0]) {
        const i = Math.floor(n.idx / PATCH_GRID);
        const j = n.idx % PATCH_GRID;
        n.targetBrightness = pat(i, j);
      }
    };

    const resetRun = () => {
      newPatchPattern();
      for (const e of edges) {
        e.tw += (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.08;
      }
      runStart = performance.now();
      nextReset = 28 + Math.random() * 12;
      cycle = 0;
      stepCount++;
    };

    const resize = () => {
      dpr = Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio || 1, 2);
      width = window.innerWidth;
      height = Math.min(380, Math.max(260, Math.floor(window.innerHeight * 0.38)));
      canvas.width = Math.floor(width * dpr);
      canvas.height = Math.floor(height * dpr);
      canvas.style.width = `${width}px`;
      canvas.style.height = `${height}px`;
      ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0);
      ctx.fillStyle = "#000000";
      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);
      buildNetwork();
      newPatchPattern();
    };

    const debouncedResize = () => {
      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);
      resizeTimer = setTimeout(resize, 180);
    };

    const fillCircle = (sx: number, sy: number, r: number, color: string, alpha: number) => {
      if (r < 0.2) return;
      ctx.globalAlpha = alpha;
      ctx.fillStyle = color;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.arc(sx, sy, r, 0, TAU);
      ctx.fill();
    };

    const fillRect = (sx: number, sy: number, size: number, color: string, alpha: number) => {
      if (size < 0.2) return;
      ctx.globalAlpha = alpha;
      ctx.fillStyle = color;
      ctx.fillRect(sx - size / 2, sy - size / 2, size, size);
    };

    const draw = (now: number) => {
      const rt = (now - runStart) * 0.001;
      if (rt > nextReset) resetRun();

      const cycleTime = rt % CYCLE_DUR;
      let phase: "fwd" | "bwd" | "update" | "settle" = "fwd";
      let phaseT = 0;
      if (cycleTime < FWD_DUR) {
        phase = "fwd";
        phaseT = cycleTime / FWD_DUR;
      } else if (cycleTime < FWD_DUR + BWD_DUR) {
        phase = "bwd";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - FWD_DUR) / BWD_DUR;
      } else if (cycleTime < FWD_DUR + BWD_DUR + UPDATE_DUR) {
        phase = "update";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - FWD_DUR - BWD_DUR) / UPDATE_DUR;
      } else {
        phase = "settle";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - FWD_DUR - BWD_DUR - UPDATE_DUR) / SETTLE_DUR;
      }

      const curCycle = Math.floor(rt / CYCLE_DUR);
      if (curCycle !== cycle) {
        cycle = curCycle;
        for (const e of edges) {
          e.tw += (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.04;
        }
      }

      // Update patch brightness (smooth transition)
      for (const n of nodes[0]) {
        n.brightness += (n.targetBrightness - n.brightness) * 0.08;
      }

      // Update edge weights
      const updateRate = phase === "update" ? 0.15 : phase === "settle" ? 0.04 : 0.01;
      for (const e of edges) e.w += (e.tw - e.w) * updateRate;

      // Sweep position for forward/backward
      let sweepPos = 0;
      let sweepDir: "fwd" | "bwd" | "none" = "none";
      if (phase === "fwd") {
        sweepPos = phaseT * (NUM_STAGES - 1);
        sweepDir = "fwd";
      } else if (phase === "bwd") {
        sweepPos = (NUM_STAGES - 1) - phaseT * (NUM_STAGES - 1);
        sweepDir = "bwd";
      }

      // Update node activations
      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        for (const n of nodes[s]) {
          if (phase === "settle") {
            n.act *= 0.82;
            n.grad *= 0.82;
          } else if (phase === "fwd") {
            const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - s);
            const bump = Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.4);
            n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.86, bump);
            n.grad *= 0.9;
          } else if (phase === "bwd") {
            const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - s);
            const bump = Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.4);
            n.grad = Math.max(n.grad * 0.86, bump);
            n.act *= 0.9;
          } else if (phase === "update") {
            n.act *= 0.88;
            n.grad *= 0.88;
          }
        }
      }

      // Fade previous frame
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.42)";
      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);

      // Project all nodes
      const projNodes: Array<{ n: NetNode; sx: number; sy: number; depth: number; scale: number }> = [];
      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        for (const n of nodes[s]) {
          const p = project(n.x, n.y, n.z);
          projNodes.push({ n, sx: p.sx, sy: p.sy, depth: p.depth, scale: p.scale });
        }
      }

      type DrawItem = { depth: number; fn: () => void };
      const drawItems: DrawItem[] = [];

      const isSweeping = sweepDir !== "none";

      // Draw edges
      for (const e of edges) {
        const a = e.a;
        const b = e.b;
        const lo = Math.min(a.stage, b.stage);
        const hi = Math.max(a.stage, b.stage);
        const mag = Math.abs(e.w);

        const pa = project(a.x, a.y, a.z);
        const pb = project(b.x, b.y, b.z);
        const avgDepth = (pa.depth + pb.depth) * 0.5;
        const depthFactor = 1 - Math.min(1, Math.max(0, avgDepth / 700)) * 0.55;
        const avgScale = (pa.scale + pb.scale) * 0.5;

        // Determine edge color based on kind
        let edgeColor: string;
        switch (e.kind) {
          case "patch": edgeColor = C.vision; break;
          case "attn": edgeColor = C.vision; break;
          case "proj": edgeColor = C.proj; break;
          case "cross-v": edgeColor = C.proj; break;
          case "cross-t": edgeColor = C.text; break;
          case "lm": edgeColor = C.lm; break;
          case "skip": edgeColor = C.skip; break;
          default: edgeColor = C.edge;
        }

        let alpha = 0.015 + mag * 0.06;
        let lw = (0.3 + mag * 0.5) * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.5, avgScale));

        if (e.kind === "skip") {
          lw = (0.25 + mag * 0.35) * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.5, avgScale));
        }

        // Sweep highlight
        let pulseColor = "";
        let pulseAlpha = 0;
        if (isSweeping && sweepPos >= lo - 0.15 && sweepPos <= hi + 0.15) {
          const span = hi - lo;
          const edgeProgress = span > 0 ? Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (sweepPos - lo) / span)) : 0.5;
          const prox = Math.sin(
            Math.min(1, Math.max(0, 1 - Math.abs(edgeProgress - 0.5) * 2)) * Math.PI * 0.5,
          );
          alpha += prox * 0.14;
          if (sweepDir === "fwd") {
            pulseColor = C.fwd;
            pulseAlpha = prox * 0.4;
          } else {
            pulseColor = C.bwd;
            pulseAlpha = prox * 0.4;
          }
        }

        // Update flash
        if (phase === "update") {
          alpha += 0.06 * (0.5 + 0.5 * Math.sin(rt * 20 + e.a.idx));
        }

        const finalAlpha = Math.min(1, alpha * depthFactor);
        const isSkip = e.kind === "skip";

        drawItems.push({
          depth: avgDepth,
          fn: () => {
            if (isSkip) {
              ctx.setLineDash(DASH);
            } else {
              ctx.setLineDash([]);
            }
            ctx.globalAlpha = finalAlpha;
            ctx.strokeStyle = edgeColor;
            ctx.lineWidth = lw;
            ctx.beginPath();
            ctx.moveTo(pa.sx, pa.sy);
            ctx.lineTo(pb.sx, pb.sy);
            ctx.stroke();

            // Pulse on edge
            if (pulseAlpha > 0.05 && mag > 0.2) {
              const span = hi - lo;
              const p = span > 0 ? Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (sweepPos - lo) / span)) : 0.5;
              const px = pa.sx + (pb.sx - pa.sx) * p;
              const py = pa.sy + (pb.sy - pa.sy) * p;
              fillCircle(px, py, 1.2 * avgScale, pulseColor, pulseAlpha);
            }
          },
        });
      }

      // Sort and draw edges
      drawItems.sort((x, y) => y.depth - x.depth);
      for (const it of drawItems) it.fn();
      ctx.setLineDash([]);

      // Draw nodes (back to front)
      projNodes.sort((x, y) => y.depth - x.depth);
      for (const { n, sx, sy, scale } of projNodes) {
        const depthFactor = 1 - Math.min(1, Math.max(0, (1 - scale) * 0.8)) * 0.4;

        if (n.stage === 0) {
          // Image patches: render as small squares with brightness
          const size = 7 * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.5, scale));
          const brightness = n.brightness;
          const alpha = (0.25 + brightness * 0.5) * depthFactor;
          
          // Glow when active
          if (n.act > 0.05) {
            fillCircle(sx, sy, size * 1.6, C.vision, n.act * 0.08 * depthFactor);
          }
          
          // Patch square
          ctx.globalAlpha = alpha;
          ctx.fillStyle = C.patch;
          ctx.fillRect(sx - size / 2, sy - size / 2, size, size);
          
          // Border
          ctx.globalAlpha = (0.15 + brightness * 0.2) * depthFactor;
          ctx.strokeStyle = C.vision;
          ctx.lineWidth = 0.5;
          ctx.strokeRect(sx - size / 2, sy - size / 2, size, size);
        } else {
          // Regular nodes: circles
          let nodeColor: string;
          switch (n.stage) {
            case 1: nodeColor = C.vision; break;
            case 2: nodeColor = C.proj; break;
            case 3: nodeColor = C.text; break;
            case 4: nodeColor = C.cross; break;
            case 5: nodeColor = C.lm; break;
            default: nodeColor = C.vision;
          }

          const baseR = 2.5 * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.5, scale));
          const actGlow = Math.max(n.act, n.grad);
          const r = baseR + actGlow * 1.5;

          if (actGlow > 0.05) {
            const glowColor = n.grad > n.act ? C.bwd : C.fwd;
            fillCircle(sx, sy, r * 2.8, glowColor, actGlow * 0.06 * depthFactor);
          }
          fillCircle(sx, sy, r, nodeColor, (0.35 + actGlow * 0.25) * depthFactor);
        }
      }

      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      animationId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);
    };

    resize();
    resetRun();
    window.addEventListener("resize", debouncedResize);
    animationId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);

    return () => {
      window.removeEventListener("resize", debouncedResize);
      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);
      cancelAnimationFrame(animationId);
    };
  }, []);

  return (
    <canvas
      ref={canvasRef}
      aria-hidden="true"
      className="pointer-events-none fixed inset-x-0 top-0 z-0 block"
    />
  );
}
```

```typescript
interface NetNode {
  stage: number;
  idx: number;
  x: number;
  y: number;
  z: number;
  act: number;
  grad: number;       // gradient activation (backward pass)
  twinkle: number;
  branch: "vision" | "lang" | "cross" | "out";
}

interface NetEdge {
  a: NetNode;
  b: NetNode;
  w: number;
  tw: number;
  kind: "embed" | "attn" | "cross" | "ffn" | "pred";
  ctrlX: number;
  ctrlY: number;
  ctrlZ: number;
}

// VLM architecture stages
// 0: Image patches (3x3 grid = 9)
// 1: Patch embeddings (9, ring)
// 2: Vision encoder (6, ring)
// 3: Text tokens (6, ring)
// 4: Cross-attention (6, ring)
// 5: Language decoder (6, ring)
// 6: Prediction (1)
const STAGE_SIZES = [9, 9, 6, 6, 6, 6, 1];
const NUM_STAGES = STAGE_SIZES.length;
const PATCH_GRID = 3; // 3x3 image patches

// Attention patterns for vision self-attention
const V_PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.8),
  (i, j) => (i === j ? 0.9 : 0.25 + 0.1 * ((i + j) % 3)),
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j - 1) ** 2) / 2.0),
];

// Cross-attention patterns (language queries, vision keys)
const C_PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-Math.abs(i * 3 - j) / 2.5),  // localized
  (i, j) => 0.3 + 0.5 * Math.abs(Math.sin((i + 1) * (j + 1) * 0.5)),  // global
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i * 2 - j) ** 2) / 3.0),  // shifted
];

const C = {
  edge: "hsl(205, 22%, 52%)",
  cross: "hsl(190, 28%, 56%)",
  pulse: "hsl(200, 35%, 68%)",
  node: "hsl(205, 24%, 58%)",
  pos: "hsl(205, 20%, 50%)",
  grad: "hsl(30, 45%, 58%)",    // warm amber for backward pass
  gradEdge: "hsl(28, 35%, 48%)",
  patch: "hsl(205, 20%, 54%)",
};

const DASH = [3, 4];
const TAU = Math.PI * 2;

// Fixed camera (no rotation) — angled to show 3D depth
const FIXED_ROT_Y = 0.48;
const CAM_TILT = -0.38;
const FOCAL = 950;
const CAM_Z = 820;
```

```typescript
const generatePatchPattern = () => {
  // Random Gaussian blob position
  const cx = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 1.5;
  const cy = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 1.5;
  const sigma = 0.5 + Math.random() * 0.5;
  for (let i = 0; i < 9; i++) {
    const row = Math.floor(i / 3);
    const col = i % 3;
    const dx = (col - 1) / 2 - cx;
    const dy = (row - 1) / 2 - cy;
    const val = Math.exp(-(dx*dx + dy*dy) / (2 * sigma * sigma));
    nodes[0][i].patchVal = val;
  }
};
```

Let me draft the complete file:

```typescript
"use client";

import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";

interface NetNode {
  stage: number;
  idx: number;
  x: number;
  y: number;
  z: number;
  act: number;
  grad: number;
  twinkle: number;
  patchVal: number;
}

interface NetEdge {
  a: NetNode;
  b: NetNode;
  w: number;
  tw: number;
  kind: "embed" | "cross" | "attn" | "ffn" | "pred";
}

// VLM (Vision-Language Model) architecture stages:
//   stage 0: image patches (3×3 grid)     — vision input
//   stage 1: vision encoder (6, ring)      — ViT self-attention + FFN
//   stage 2: text tokens (6, ring)         — language input
//   stage 3: cross-attention (6, ring)     — language queries ← vision keys
//   stage 4: language decoder (6, ring)    — LM self-attention + FFN
//   stage 5: next-token prediction (1)     — output
const STAGE_SIZES = [9, 6, 6, 6, 6, 1];
const NUM_STAGES = STAGE_SIZES.length;
const PATCH_GRID = 3;
const N_TOK = 6;

// Vision self-attention patterns
const V_PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.8),
  (i, j) => (i === j ? 0.9 : 0.22 + 0.1 * ((i + j) % 3)),
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j - 1) ** 2) / 2.2),
];

// Cross-attention patterns (language query i, vision key j)
const C_PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 2.0),
  (i, j) => 0.3 + 0.5 * Math.abs(Math.sin((i + 1) * (j + 1) * 0.5)),
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i * 2 - j) ** 2) / 3.5),
  (i, j) => (j >= i ? Math.exp(-(j - i) / 2.0) : 0.15),
];

const C = {
  edge: "hsl(205, 22%, 52%)",
  cross: "hsl(190, 30%, 56%)",
  pulse: "hsl(200, 35%, 70%)",
  node: "hsl(205, 24%, 58%)",
  pos: "hsl(205, 20%, 50%)",
  grad: "hsl(30, 50%, 60%)",     // warm amber for backward gradients
  gradPulse: "hsl(32, 55%, 65%)",
  patch: "hsl(205, 20%, 54%)",
};

const DASH = [3, 4];
const TAU = Math.PI * 2;

// Fixed camera — angled view showing 3D depth, no rotation
const FIXED_ROT_Y = 0.48;
const CAM_TILT = -0.38;
const FOCAL = 950;
const CAM_Z = 820;

// Training phases
const FWD_DUR = 3.0;
const BWD_DUR = 2.6;
const UPD_DUR = 0.9;
const CYCLE_DUR = FWD_DUR + BWD_DUR + UPD_DUR;

export default function ParticleField() {
  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    const canvas = canvasRef.current;
    if (!canvas) return;
    const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d", { alpha: true });
    if (!ctx) return;

    let animationId = 0;
    let width = 0;
    let height = 0;
    let dpr = 1;
    let resizeTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;

    let nodes: NetNode[][] = [];
    let edges: NetEdge[] = [];
    let runStart = performance.now();
    let nextReset = 28 + Math.random() * 12;
    let cycle = 0;
    let vPatIdx = Math.floor(Math.random() * V_PATTERNS.length);
    let cPatIdx = Math.floor(Math.random() * C_PATTERNS.length);

    const randn = () =>
      (Math.random() + Math.random() + Math.random() - 1.5) * 0.5;

    let stageSpacingX = 130;
    let ringRadius = 70;
    let patchSpacing = 42;
    let layoutCy = 0;

    // 3D point → screen. Fixed rotation, no camera movement.
    const project = (wx: number, wy: number, wz: number) => {
      const cosY = Math.cos(FIXED_ROT_Y);
      const sinY = Math.sin(FIXED_ROT_Y);
      const rx = wx * cosY - wz * sinY;
      const rz = wx * sinY + wz * cosY;
      const cosT = Math.cos(CAM_TILT);
      const sinT = Math.sin(CAM_TILT);
      const ty = wy * cosT - rz * sinT;
      const tz = wy * sinT + rz * cosT;
      const denom = CAM_Z + tz;
      const safeDenom = denom > 1 ? denom : 1;
      const scale = FOCAL / safeDenom;
      return {
        sx: width / 2 + rx * scale,
        sy: layoutCy + ty * scale,
        depth: tz,
        scale,
      };
    };

    const buildNetwork = () => {
      nodes = [];
      edges = [];

      const availW = width - 280;
      stageSpacingX = Math.max(105, Math.min(140, availW / (NUM_STAGES - 1)));
      const h = height;
      ringRadius = Math.min(65, h * 0.15);
      patchSpacing = Math.min(40, h * 0.085);
      layoutCy = height * 0.5;

      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        const n = STAGE_SIZES[s];
        const stageNodes: NetNode[] = [];
        const wx = (s - (NUM_STAGES - 1) / 2) * stageSpacingX;

        if (s === 0) {
          // Image patches: 3×3 grid in Y-Z plane
          for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            const row = Math.floor(i / PATCH_GRID);
            const col = i % PATCH_GRID;
            stageNodes.push({
              stage: s,
              idx: i,
              x: wx,
              y: (row - 1) * patchSpacing,
              z: (col - 1) * patchSpacing,
              act: 0,
              grad: 0,
              twinkle: Math.random() * TAU,
              patchVal: 0.3 + Math.random() * 0.4,
            });
          }
        } else if (s === NUM_STAGES - 1) {
          // Single prediction node
          stageNodes.push({
            stage: s,
            idx: 0,
            x: wx,
            y: 0,
            z: 0,
            act: 0,
            grad: 0,
            twinkle: 0,
            patchVal: 0,
          });
        } else {
          // Ring layout
          for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            const ang = (i / n) * TAU;
            stageNodes.push({
              stage: s,
              idx: i,
              x: wx,
              y: Math.cos(ang) * ringRadius,
              z: Math.sin(ang) * ringRadius,
              act: 0,
              grad: 0,
              twinkle: Math.random() * TAU,
              patchVal: 0,
            });
          }
        }
        nodes.push(stageNodes);
      }

      const addEdge = (a: NetNode, b: NetNode, w: number, kind: NetEdge["kind"]) => {
        edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind });
      };

      // Stage 0→1: patch embedding (each patch → multiple encoder nodes)
      for (const a of nodes[0]) for (const b of nodes[1]) {
        addEdge(a, b, 0.3 + Math.random() * 0.3, "embed");
      }

      // Stage 1→3: cross-attention (vision keys → language queries)
      const cpat = C_PATTERNS[cPatIdx];
      for (const a of nodes[1]) for (const b of nodes[3]) {
        addEdge(a, b, cpat(b.idx, a.idx), "cross");
      }

      // Stage 2→3: text attention (text tokens → cross-attention)
      const vpat = V_PATTERNS[vPatIdx];
      for (const a of nodes[2]) for (const b of nodes[3]) {
        addEdge(a, b, vpat(a.idx, b.idx), "attn");
      }

      // Stage 3→4: FFN
      for (const a of nodes[3]) for (const b of nodes[4]) {
        addEdge(a, b, randn() * 0.4 + 0.3, "ffn");
      }

      // Stage 4→5: prediction
      for (const a of nodes[4]) {
        addEdge(a, nodes[5][0], 0.4 + Math.random() * 0.3, "pred");
      }
    };

    const generatePatchPattern = () => {
      const cx = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 1.5;
      const cy = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 1.5;
      const sigma = 0.5 + Math.random() * 0.4;
      for (let i = 0; i < STAGE_SIZES[0]; i++) {
        const row = Math.floor(i / PATCH_GRID);
        const col = i % PATCH_GRID;
        const dx = (col - 1) / 1.5 - cx;
        const dy = (row - 1) / 1.5 - cy;
        const val = Math.exp(-(dx * dx + dy * dy) / (2 * sigma * sigma));
        nodes[0][i].patchVal = 0.15 + val * 0.75;
      }
    };

    const resetRun = () => {
      vPatIdx = (vPatIdx + 1 + Math.floor(Math.random() * (V_PATTERNS.length - 1))) % V_PATTERNS.length;
      cPatIdx = (cPatIdx + 1 + Math.floor(Math.random() * (C_PATTERNS.length - 1))) % C_PATTERNS.length;
      const cpat = C_PATTERNS[cPatIdx];
      const vpat = V_PATTERNS[vPatIdx];
      for (const e of edges) {
        if (e.kind === "cross") e.tw = cpat(e.b.idx, e.a.idx);
        else if (e.kind === "attn") e.tw = vpat(e.a.idx, e.b.idx);
        else e.tw += (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.05;
      }
      generatePatchPattern();
      runStart = performance.now();
      nextReset = 28 + Math.random() * 12;
      cycle = 0;
    };

    const resize = () => {
      dpr = Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio || 1, 2);
      width = window.innerWidth;
      height = Math.min(380, Math.max(260, Math.floor(window.innerHeight * 0.38)));
      canvas.width = Math.floor(width * dpr);
      canvas.height = Math.floor(height * dpr);
      canvas.style.width = `${width}px`;
      canvas.style.height = `${height}px`;
      ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0);
      ctx.fillStyle = "#000000";
      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);
      buildNetwork();
      generatePatchPattern();
    };

    const debouncedResize = () => {
      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);
      resizeTimer = setTimeout(resize, 180);
    };

    const fillCircle = (sx: number, sy: number, r: number, color: string, alpha: number) => {
      if (r < 0.2) return;
      ctx.globalAlpha = alpha;
      ctx.fillStyle = color;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.arc(sx, sy, r, 0, TAU);
      ctx.fill();
    };

    const draw = (now: number) => {
      const rt = (now - runStart) * 0.001;
      if (rt > nextReset) resetRun();

      const cycleTime = rt % CYCLE_DUR;
      let phase: "fwd" | "bwd" | "upd" = "fwd";
      let phaseT = 0;
      if (cycleTime < FWD_DUR) {
        phase = "fwd";
        phaseT = cycleTime / FWD_DUR;
      } else if (cycleTime < FWD_DUR + BWD_DUR) {
        phase = "bwd";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - FWD_DUR) / BWD_DUR;
      } else {
        phase = "upd";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - FWD_DUR - BWD_DUR) / UPD_DUR;
      }

      const curCycle = Math.floor(rt / CYCLE_DUR);
      if (curCycle !== cycle) {
        cycle = curCycle;
        for (const e of edges) {
          if (e.kind !== "cross" && e.kind !== "attn") e.tw += (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.03;
        }
      }

      const updateRate = phase === "upd" ? 0.08 : 0.012;
      for (const e of edges) e.w += (e.tw - e.w) * updateRate;

      // Sweep position for forward/backward
      let sweepPos = 0;
      let sweepDir: "fwd" | "bwd" | "none" = "none";
      if (phase === "fwd") {
        sweepPos = phaseT * (NUM_STAGES - 1);
        sweepDir = "fwd";
      } else if (phase === "bwd") {
        sweepPos = (1 - phaseT) * (NUM_STAGES - 1);
        sweepDir = "bwd";
      } else {
        sweepPos = -1; // no sweep during update
        sweepDir = "none";
      }

      // Update node activations
      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - s);
        const bump = Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.5);
        for (const n of nodes[s]) {
          if (phase === "upd") {
            n.act *= 0.82;
            n.grad *= 0.82;
          } else if (phase === "fwd") {
            n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, bump);
            n.grad *= 0.88;
          } else {
            // backward
            n.grad = Math.max(n.grad * 0.84, bump);
            n.act *= 0.88;
          }
        }
      }

      // Fade previous frame
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.42)";
      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);

      // Project all nodes
      const projNodes: Array<{ n: NetNode; sx: number; sy: number; depth: number; scale: number }> = [];
      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        for (const n of nodes[s]) {
          const p = project(n.x, n.y, n.z);
          projNodes.push({ n, sx: p.sx, sy: p.sy, depth: p.depth, scale: p.scale });
        }
      }

      const isSweeping = sweepDir !== "none";

      type DrawItem = { depth: number; fn: () => void };
      const drawItems: DrawItem[] = [];

      for (const e of edges) {
        const a = e.a;
        const b = e.b;
        const lo = a.stage;
        const hi = b.stage;
        const mag = Math.abs(e.w);
        
        // Determine if this edge is being swept
        const sweeping = isSweeping && sweepPos >= lo - 0.1 && sweepPos <= hi + 0.1;

        let alpha = 0.02 + mag * 0.06;
        if (sweeping) {
          const span = hi - lo;
          const edgeProgress = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (sweepPos - lo) / span));
          const prox = Math.sin(
            Math.min(1, Math.max(0, 1 - Math.abs(edgeProgress - 0.5) * 2)) * Math.PI * 0.5,
          );
          alpha += prox * 0.14;
        }

        // Weight update flash
        if (phase === "upd") {
          const flash = Math.sin(phaseT * Math.PI) * (0.3 + mag * 0.3);
          alpha += flash * 0.1;
        }

        const pa = project(a.x, a.y, a.z);
        const pb = project(b.x, b.y, b.z);
        const avgDepth = (pa.depth + pb.depth) * 0.5;
        const depthFactor = 1 - Math.min(1, Math.max(0, avgDepth / 700)) * 0.55;
        const finalAlpha = Math.min(1, alpha * depthFactor);
        const avgScale = (pa.scale + pb.scale) * 0.5;
        
        const edgeColor = e.kind === "cross" ? C.cross : C.edge;
        const lw = (0.35 + mag * 0.6) * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.5, avgScale));
        
        // For cross edges, slightly thicker
        const lwBoost = e.kind === "cross" ? 1.15 : 1.0;

        drawItems.push({
          depth: avgDepth,
          fn: () => {
            ctx.setLineDash([]);
            ctx.globalAlpha = finalAlpha;
            ctx.strokeStyle = edgeColor;
            ctx.lineWidth = lw * lwBoost;
            ctx.beginPath();
            ctx.moveTo(pa.sx, pa.sy);
            ctx.lineTo(pb.sx, pb.sy);
            ctx.stroke();

            // Pulse particle along edge during sweep
            if (sweeping && mag > 0.2) {
              const span = hi - lo;
              let edgeProgress = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (sweepPos - lo) / span));
              // For backward sweep, reverse the direction
              if (sweepDir === "bwd") edgeProgress = 1 - edgeProgress;
              const px = pa.sx + (pb.sx - pa.sx) * edgeProgress;
              const py = pa.sy + (pb.sy - pa.sy) * edgeProgress;
              const pulseColor = sweepDir === "bwd" ? C.gradPulse : C.pulse;
              fillCircle(px, py, 1.2 * avgScale, pulseColor, 0.4);
            }
          },
        });
      }

      drawItems.sort((x, y) => y.depth - x.depth);
      for (const it of drawItems) it.fn();
      ctx.setLineDash([]);

      // Nodes — back-to-front
      projNodes.sort((x, y) => y.depth - x.depth);
      for (const { n, sx, sy, scale } of projNodes) {
        const isPrediction = n.stage === NUM_STAGES - 1;
        const isPatch = n.stage === 0;
        const baseR = (isPrediction ? 4.0 : isPatch ? 2.5 : 2.4) * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.5, scale));
        
        const inputBoost = isPatch ? n.patchVal * (0.15 + 0.1 * Math.sin(rt * 0.8 + n.twinkle)) : 0;
        const glow = Math.max(n.act, n.grad, Math.max(0, inputBoost));
        const r = baseR + glow * 1.5;
        const depthFactor = 1 - Math.min(1, Math.max(0, (1 - scale) * 0.8)) * 0.4;

        // Glow
        if (glow > 0.05) {
          const glowColor = n.grad > n.act ? C.grad : C.node;
          fillCircle(sx, sy, r * 2.8, glowColor, glow * 0.08 * depthFactor);
        }

        // Node body — color depends on whether it's showing forward activation or backward gradient
        const bodyColor = n.grad > n.act && n.grad > 0.05 ? C.grad : C.node;
        const bodyAlpha = (0.3 + glow * 0.22) * depthFactor;
        
        // For patches, brightness modulated by patchVal
        if (isPatch) {
          fillCircle(sx, sy, r, C.patch, (0.2 + n.patchVal * 0.25 + glow * 0.15) * depthFactor);
        } else {
          fillCircle(sx, sy, r, bodyColor, bodyAlpha);
        }

        // For prediction node, extra glow
        if (isPrediction && glow > 0.05) {
          fillCircle(sx, sy, r * 3.5, C.pulse, glow * 0.06 * depthFactor);
        }
      }

      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      animationId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);
    };

    resize();
    resetRun();
    window.addEventListener("resize", debouncedResize);
    animationId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);

    return () => {
      window.removeEventListener("resize", debouncedResize);
      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);
      cancelAnimationFrame(animationId);
    };
  }, []);

  return (
    <canvas
      ref={canvasRef}
      aria-hidden="true"
      className="pointer-events-none fixed inset-x-0 top-0 z-0 block"
    />
  );
}
```

```typescript
"use client";

import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";

interface NetNode {
  stage: number;
  idx: number;
  x: number;
  y: number;
  z: number;
  act: number;
  grad: number;       // gradient activation (backward pass)
  twinkle: number;
  branch: "vision" | "lang" | "cross" | "out";
}

interface NetEdge {
  a: NetNode;
  b: NetNode;
  w: number;
  tw: number;
  kind: "embed" | "attn" | "cross" | "ffn" | "pred";
  ctrlX: number;
  ctrlY: number;
  ctrlZ: number;
}

// VLM architecture stages
// 0: Image patches (3x3 grid = 9)
// 1: Patch embeddings (9, ring)
// 2: Vision encoder (6, ring)
// 3: Text tokens (6, ring)
// 4: Cross-attention (6, ring)
// 5: Language decoder (6, ring)
// 6: Prediction (1)
const STAGE_SIZES = [9, 9, 6, 6, 6, 6, 1];
const NUM_STAGES = STAGE_SIZES.length;
const PATCH_GRID = 3; // 3x3 image patches

// Attention patterns for vision self-attention
const V_PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.8),
  (i, j) => (i === j ? 0.9 : 0.25 + 0.1 * ((i + j) % 3)),
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j - 1) ** 2) / 2.0),
];

// Cross-attention patterns (language queries, vision keys)
const C_PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-Math.abs(i * 3 - j) / 2.5),  // localized
  (i, j) => 0.3 + 0.5 * Math.abs(Math.sin((i + 1) * (j + 1) * 0.5)),  // global
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i * 2 - j) ** 2) / 3.0),  // shifted
];

const C = {
  edge: "hsl(205, 22%, 52%)",
  cross: "hsl(190, 28%, 56%)",
  pulse: "hsl(200, 35%, 68%)",
  node: "hsl(205, 24%, 58%)",
  pos: "hsl(205, 20%, 50%)",
  grad: "hsl(30, 45%, 58%)",    // warm amber for backward pass
  gradEdge: "hsl(28, 35%, 48%)",
  patch: "hsl(205, 20%, 54%)",
};

const DASH = [3, 4];
const TAU = Math.PI * 2;

// Fixed camera (no rotation) — angled to show 3D depth
const FIXED_ROT_Y = 0.48;
const CAM_TILT = -0.38;
const FOCAL = 950;
const CAM_Z = 820;
```

Here's my approach for the patch pattern:
```typescript
const generatePatchPattern = () => {
  // Random Gaussian blob position
  const cx = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 1.5;
  const cy = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 1.5;
  const sigma = 0.5 + Math.random() * 0.5;
  for (let i = 0; i < 9; i++) {
    const row = Math.floor(i / 3);
    const col = i % 3;
    const dx = (col - 1) / 2 - cx;
    const dy = (row - 1) / 2 - cy;
    const val = Math.exp(-(dx*dx + dy*dy) / (2 * sigma * sigma));
    nodes[0][i].patchVal = val;
  }
};
```

This creates a blob of brightness at a random position in the 3x3 grid, like a simple "image" being processed.
